CVE-2024-52530.title: GNOME libsoup before 3.6.0 allows HTTP request smuggling in some  ...
CVE-2024-52530.summary: GNOME libsoup before 3.6.0 allows HTTP request smuggling in some configurations because '\0' characters at the end of header names are ignored, i.e., a "Transfer-Encoding\0: chunked" header is treated the same as a "Transfer-Encoding: chunked" header.

CVE-2024-52531.title: GNOME libsoup before 3.6.1 allows a buffer overflow in applicatio ...
CVE-2024-52531.summary: GNOME libsoup before 3.6.1 allows a buffer overflow in applications that perform conversion to UTF-8 in soup_header_parse_param_list_strict. There is a plausible way to reach this remotely via soup_message_headers_get_content_type (e.g., an application may want to retrieve the content type of a request or response).

CVE-2024-52532.title: GNOME libsoup before 3.6.1 has an infinite loop, and memory consu ...
CVE-2024-52532.summary: GNOME libsoup before 3.6.1 has an infinite loop, and memory consumption. during the reading of certain patterns of WebSocket data from clients.

CVE-2025-2784.title: A flaw was found in libsoup. The package is vulnerable to a heap  ...
CVE-2025-2784.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup. The package is vulnerable to a heap buffer over-read when sniffing content via the skip_insight_whitespace() function. Libsoup clients may read one byte out-of-bounds in response to a crafted HTTP response by an HTTP server.

CVE-2025-32049.title: A flaw was found in libsoup. The SoupWebsocketConnection may acce ...
CVE-2025-32049.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup. The SoupWebsocketConnection may accept a large WebSocket message, which may cause libsoup to allocate memory and lead to a denial of service (DoS).

CVE-2025-32050.title: A flaw was found in libsoup. The libsoup append_param_quoted() fu ...
CVE-2025-32050.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup. The libsoup append_param_quoted() function may contain an overflow bug resulting in a buffer under-read.

CVE-2025-32052.title: A flaw was found in libsoup. A vulnerability in the sniff_unknown ...
CVE-2025-32052.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup. A vulnerability in the sniff_unknown() function may lead to heap buffer over-read.

CVE-2025-32053.title: A flaw was found in libsoup. A vulnerability in sniff_feed_or_htm ...
CVE-2025-32053.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup. A vulnerability in sniff_feed_or_html() and skip_insignificant_space() functions may lead to a heap buffer over-read.

CVE-2025-32906.title: A flaw was found in libsoup, where the soup_headers_parse_request ...
CVE-2025-32906.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup, where the soup_headers_parse_request() function may be vulnerable to an out-of-bound read. This flaw allows a malicious user to use a specially crafted HTTP request to crash the HTTP server.

CVE-2025-32907.title: A flaw was found in libsoup. The implementation of HTTP range req ...
CVE-2025-32907.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup. The implementation of HTTP range requests is vulnerable to a resource consumption attack. This flaw allows a malicious client to request the same range many times in a single HTTP request, causing the server to use large amounts of memory. This does not allow for a full denial of service.

CVE-2025-32909.title: A flaw was found in libsoup. SoupContentSniffer may be vulnerable ...
CVE-2025-32909.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup. SoupContentSniffer may be vulnerable to a NULL pointer dereference in the sniff_mp4 function. The HTTP server may cause the libsoup client to crash.

CVE-2025-32910.title: A flaw was found in libsoup, where soup_auth_digest_authenticate( ...
CVE-2025-32910.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup, where soup_auth_digest_authenticate() is vulnerable to a NULL pointer dereference. This issue may cause the libsoup client to crash.

CVE-2025-32911.title: A use-after-free type vulnerability was found in libsoup, in the  ...
CVE-2025-32911.summary: A use-after-free type vulnerability was found in libsoup, in the soup_message_headers_get_content_disposition() function. This flaw allows a malicious HTTP client to cause memory corruption in the libsoup server.

CVE-2025-32912.title: A flaw was found in libsoup, where SoupAuthDigest is vulnerable t ...
CVE-2025-32912.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup, where SoupAuthDigest is vulnerable to a NULL pointer dereference. The HTTP server may cause the libsoup client to crash.

CVE-2025-32913.title: A flaw was found in libsoup, where the soup_message_headers_get_c ...
CVE-2025-32913.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup, where the soup_message_headers_get_content_disposition() function is vulnerable to a NULL pointer dereference. This flaw allows a malicious HTTP peer to crash a libsoup client or server that uses this function.

CVE-2025-32914.title: A flaw was found in libsoup, where the soup_multipart_new_from_me ...
CVE-2025-32914.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup, where the soup_multipart_new_from_message() function is vulnerable to an out-of-bounds read. This flaw allows a malicious HTTP client to induce the libsoup server to read out of bounds.

CVE-2025-4035.title: A flaw was found in libsoup. When handling cookies, libsoup clien ...
CVE-2025-4035.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup. When handling cookies, libsoup clients mistakenly allow cookies to be set for public suffix domains if the domain contains at least two components and includes an uppercase character. This bypasses public suffix protections and could allow a malicious website to set cookies for domains it does not own, potentially leading to integrity issues such as session fixation.

CVE-2025-4476.title: A denial-of-service vulnerability has been identified in the libs ...
CVE-2025-4476.summary: A denial-of-service vulnerability has been identified in the libsoup HTTP client library. This flaw can be triggered when a libsoup client receives a 401 (Unauthorized) HTTP response containing a specifically crafted domain parameter within the WWW-Authenticate header. Processing this malformed header can lead to a crash of the client application using libsoup. An attacker could exploit this by setting up a malicious HTTP server. If a user's application using the vulnerable libsoup library connects to this malicious server, it could result in a denial-of-service. Successful exploitation requires tricking a user's client application into connecting to the attacker's malicious server.

CVE-2025-46420.title: A flaw was found in libsoup. It is vulnerable to memory leaks in  ...
CVE-2025-46420.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup. It is vulnerable to memory leaks in the soup_header_parse_quality_list() function when parsing a quality list that contains elements with all zeroes.

CVE-2025-46421.title: A flaw was found in libsoup. When libsoup clients encounter an HT ...
CVE-2025-46421.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup. When libsoup clients encounter an HTTP redirect, they mistakenly send the HTTP Authorization header to the new host that the redirection points to. This allows the new host to impersonate the user to the original host that issued the redirect.

CVE-2025-4945.title: A flaw was found in the cookie parsing logic of the libsoup HTTP  ...
CVE-2025-4945.summary: A flaw was found in the cookie parsing logic of the libsoup HTTP library, used in GNOME applications and other software. The vulnerability arises when processing the expiration date of cookies, where a specially crafted value can trigger an integer overflow. This may result in undefined behavior, allowing an attacker to bypass cookie expiration logic, causing persistent or unintended cookie behavior. The issue stems from improper validation of large integer inputs during date arithmetic operations within the cookie parsing routines.

CVE-2025-4948.title: A flaw was found in the soup_multipart_new_from_message() functio ...
CVE-2025-4948.summary: A flaw was found in the soup_multipart_new_from_message() function of the libsoup HTTP library, which is commonly used by GNOME and other applications to handle web communications. The issue occurs when the library processes specially crafted multipart messages. Due to improper validation, an internal calculation can go wrong, leading to an integer underflow. This can cause the program to access invalid memory and crash. As a result, any application or server using libsoup could be forced to exit unexpectedly, creating a denial-of-service (DoS) risk.

CVE-2025-4969.title: A vulnerability was found in the libsoup package. This flaw stems ...
CVE-2025-4969.summary: A vulnerability was found in the libsoup package. This flaw stems from its failure to correctly verify the termination of multipart HTTP messages. This can allow a remote attacker to send a specially crafted multipart HTTP body, causing the libsoup-consuming server to read beyond its allocated memory boundaries (out-of-bounds read).

CVE-2025-8197.title: Rejected reason: Maintainers have included reasons at https://git ...
CVE-2025-8197.summary: Rejected reason: Maintainers have included reasons at https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libsoup/-/issues/465

CVE-2024-52532.title: GNOME libsoup before 3.6.1 has an infinite loop, and memory consu ...
CVE-2024-52532.summary: GNOME libsoup before 3.6.1 has an infinite loop, and memory consumption. during the reading of certain patterns of WebSocket data from clients.

CVE-2025-11021.title: A flaw was found in the cookie date handling logic of the libsoup ...
CVE-2025-11021.summary: A flaw was found in the cookie date handling logic of the libsoup HTTP library, widely used by GNOME and other applications for web communication. When processing cookies with specially crafted expiration dates, the library may perform an out-of-bounds memory read. This flaw could result in unintended disclosure of memory contents, potentially exposing sensitive information from the process using libsoup.

CVE-2025-12105.title: A flaw was found in the asynchronous message queue handling of th ...
CVE-2025-12105.summary: A flaw was found in the asynchronous message queue handling of the libsoup library, widely used by GNOME and WebKit-based applications to manage HTTP/2 communications. When network operations are aborted at specific timing intervals, an internal message queue item may be freed twice due to missing state synchronization. This leads to a use-after-free memory access, potentially crashing the affected application. Attackers could exploit this behavior remotely by triggering specific HTTP/2 read and cancel sequences, resulting in a denial-of-service condition.

CVE-2025-9901.title: A flaw was found in libsoup’s caching mechanism, SoupCache, where ...
CVE-2025-9901.summary: A flaw was found in libsoup’s caching mechanism, SoupCache, where the HTTP Vary header is ignored when evaluating cached responses. This header ensures that responses vary appropriately based on request headers such as language or authentication. Without this check, cached content can be incorrectly reused across different requests, potentially exposing sensitive user information. While the issue is unlikely to affect everyday desktop use, it could result in confidentiality breaches in proxy or multi-user environments.
